page: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-96GB is a compute-focused virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system allocates shared CPU resources with one thread per core. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 1.5 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 200 GB SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this hardware configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-threaded and instruction-heavy capabilities, but limited single-core performance. The server achieves strong results in the Passmark CPU suite, including the extended instructions, encryption, and physics tests, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench ray tracing. Conversely, single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by a poor rating in stress-ng single-core CPU testing. Memory bandwidth and standard database operations, such as Redis SET operations, perform within average ranges. Large-model LLM inference prompt processing is weak, though smaller model inference remains average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server provides a high density of physical compute cores relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for parallelized, compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive RAM allocations. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data decompression, ray tracing, physics simulations, and parallel software compilation. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases or single-threaded applications due to its low memory-to-core ratio and lower single-core benchmark performance.
description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-96GB is a compute-focused virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing 1.5 GB of memory per core, and includes a bundled 200 GB local SSD. Benchmark data shows strong performance in multi-threaded operations, including Passmark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and ray tracing. However, single-core performance is poor, and large-model LLM inference is weak. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads that do not demand high memory-to-core ratios. It is best suited for tasks such as software compilation, physics simulations, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-96GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, offering strong multi-threaded performance for parallel compute workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud HICPU-64xCPU-96GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded compute tasks.
tagline: A high-core-density virtual server featuring sixty-four AMD EPYC vCPUs and bundled SSD storage for highly parallelized compute workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs with x86_64 architecture
96.0 GB of RAM providing 1.5 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded performance in physics and encryption benchmarks
Best suited for parallel software compilation and multi-threaded decompression
